generated_oml:
// C1: Physical Sensing Components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor providing point‑cloud frames on the vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARDataTransmitted
]

instance VehicleStateTelemetrySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle‑state telemetry sensor delivering speed, orientation and GPS data"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorFeedbackSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor feedback sensor reporting wheel torque and RPM"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorFeedbackDataTransmitted
]

instance SteeringStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering status sensor exposing current steering angle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringStatusDataTransmitted
]

instance ServoStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Servo status sensor exposing actuator position and health"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ServoStatusDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical‑to‑Virtual Interaction (Data Transmitted)
instance LiDARDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance VehicleStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateTelemetrySensor
]

instance MotorFeedbackDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorFeedbackSensor
]

instance SteeringStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringStatusSensor
]

instance ServoStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ServoStatusSensor
]

// C5: Data Instances
instance LiDARRawData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point‑cloud frame received over UDP"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringFE
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed vehicle state (speed, pose, GPS) after unit conversion"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationPreProcessingFE, ACCAlgorithmFE
]

instance MotorFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor torque and RPM values after translation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorFeedbackDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCAlgorithmFE
]

instance SteeringStatusData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Current steering angle after translation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringStatusDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationPreProcessingFE
]

instance ServoStatusData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Servo position and health information after translation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ServoStatusDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationPreProcessingFE
]

// C2: Acting Component
instance VehicleActuationComponent : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuation software component mapped to a Functional Entity that sends speed commands to the vehicle’s actuators"
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalTwinEntity
]

// C11: Enablers (Functional Entities)
instance PointCloudClusteringFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity that clusters and tracks objects from LiDAR point clouds"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinEntity
]

instance VisualizationPreProcessingFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity that prepares vehicle state and sensor data for 3D visualization and UI rendering"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinEntity
]

instance ACCAlgorithmFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity implementing the Adaptive Cruise Control algorithm (lead‑vehicle detection and desired‑speed calculation)"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinEntity
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides real‑time 3D visualization of vehicle state, projected path and detected object tracks"
    DTDFVocab:provides MonitoringInsight
    base:isContainedIn UserEntity
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executes ACC algorithm in the twin and returns speed command to the physical vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCControlInsight, SpeedCommandAction
    base:isContainedIn UserEntity
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables operators to enable/disable ACC, set desired speed and issue emergency‑brake commands"
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCEnableAction, DesiredSpeedSetAction, EmergencyBrakeAction
    base:isContainedIn UserEntity
]

// C17: Insights
instance MonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight that the UI shows live vehicle state, projected path and tracked objects"
]

instance ACCControlInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight that the ACC algorithm has produced a speed command based on traffic conditions"
]

// C17: Actions
instance SpeedCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action sending the computed speed command to the VehicleActuationComponent"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ACCEnableAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manual action to enable or disable Adaptive Cruise Control via the UI"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DesiredSpeedSetAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manual action allowing the operator to set a target cruising speed"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manual emergency‑brake command issued from the UI to the vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C18: Environment (Entities)
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical Twin Entity hosting sensors and actuation components"
    base:contains LiDARSensor, VehicleStateTelemetrySensor, MotorFeedbackSensor, SteeringStatusSensor, ServoStatusSensor, VehicleActuationComponent
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:DigitalTwin [
    base:desc "Digital Twin Entity hosting functional enablers"
    base:contains PointCloudClusteringFE, VisualizationPreProcessingFE, ACCAlgorithmFE
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "User Entity exposing services to operators"
    base:contains DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DTS sends control commands back to the vehicle over the same Wi‑Fi UDP link. Commands include a desired speed set‑point calculated by the off‑loaded ACC algorithm, ACC enable/disable flags, and emergency‑brake trigger messages. These command messages are formatted by the Data Translation FE to match the vehicle’s actuation interface and are received by the Actuation FE, which drives the motor, steering and servo actuators. Execution confirmation is observed through subsequent vehicle state telemetry that is fed back into the monitoring loop."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The system requires a shared notion of time among the distributed devices to support computation offloading. Clock synchronization must achieve at least 100 ms accuracy, which is the stated hardware limitation; standards for safety‑critical vehicular applications often demand tighter (<100 ms) sync, but this DT operates with a maximum allowed offset of 100 ms. No further latency or service‑level time‑scale details are provided."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "Orchestration is achieved by mapping software components to Functional Entities and implementing each FE as an individual ROS 2 package containing ROS 2 nodes. ROS 2 middleware provides the inter‑entity communication, data exchange and coordination, while the ISO 23247 reference architecture defines the roles of the Physical Twin, Digital Twin and User entities. Resource management is implicit in the deployment of each entity on separate hardware, and system‑wide coordination relies on ROS 2 topic/service interactions together with the 100 ms time‑synchronisation constraint."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) follows the ISO 23247 lifecycle approach. Development starts with a requirements definition phase, proceeds to an architectural design phase where key software components are identified (Table I) and mapped onto Functional Entities (FEs) defined by the ISO 23247 reference architecture. Implementation is realised by instantiating each FE as an individual ROS 2 package containing one or more ROS 2 nodes. Deployment places each ISO entity on a distinct hardware component (e.g., Physical Twin Entity on the vehicle’s compute platform, Digital Twin Entity on a processing unit, User Entity on a HMI device). During operation the User Entity provides monitoring and control interfaces, while the Data Collection and Device Control Entity gathers sensor data and actuates the Observable Manufacturing Element (OME). Transition between phases is governed by the entity‑based structure of ISO 23247, ensuring traceability from design artefacts to deployed services, and the overall lifecycle is managed as a product‑lifecycle‑management tool within the ISO framework."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The system requires clock synchronization across devices with an accuracy of 100 ms, driven by hardware limitations and the need to meet safety‑critical vehicular application standards that often mandate stricter synchronization. This time‑synchronization constraint is a primary fidelity consideration for ensuring coherent data acquisition and actuation timing within the Digital Twin. No additional model validation, uncertainty quantification, or error‑correction mechanisms are described in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DTS uses a hybrid network architecture: intra‑entity software components communicate via ROS2 (DDS) on a local network, while inter‑entity communication between the Physical Twin and the Digital Twin occurs over a Wi‑Fi (IEEE 802.11) network using UDP transport. The connection is designed to provide low latency for real‑time monitoring and control, though specific bandwidth, redundancy, or security mechanisms are not detailed in the documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The ACC service is off‑loaded from the vehicle to the DT, which may be executed on external compute resources such as a cloud platform (the documents reference 'in the cloud' as an example). The DT therefore provides the computational capacity needed for the ACC algorithm and stores collected data for reporting, but detailed specifications of storage size, scaling mechanisms, deployment models or reliability guarantees are not provided."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The system adopts the ISO 23247 digital‑twin framework for manufacturing, specifically leveraging Part 2 (Reference Architecture). It implements the entity‑based model defined by ISO 23247, including Observable Manufacturing Elements (OME), Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity and User Entity, each composed of Functional Entities. The software components are mapped onto ROS 2 packages to realize these entities, and a Physical Twin Entity is introduced as a deviation from the standard. No other industry standards, certification requirements, or interoperability specifications are cited."
]
